title: The End of the Bottleneck
concept: By using a dynamic context vector instead of a fixed bottle vector, attention allows translation of very long sentences. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Attention Mechanics.'

exercises.prompt: What was the main limitation of early translation models before attention?

options.text: Squeezing everything into a single fixed vector
Using too many dictionaries at once

exercises.explain: Older models squeezed everything into one fixed vector, which limited their ability to translate long sentences.

cards.title: The Sentence Jam
cards.body: Imagine trying to read a 100-word sentence, closing your eyes, and translating it perfectly. Sounds impossible, right? That is exactly what old AI models tried to do before attention came along!

cards.title: What is Attention?
cards.body: In old translation models, the entire input sentence had to squeeze through a single, fixed-size 'bottleneck' vector. Attention solves this by creating a dynamic context vector for every single step, focusing on different parts of the input as needed.

cards.title: The Highlight Pen
cards.body: Think of a traditional translator trying to memorize a whole book paragraph by paragraph before writing it down in another language. They will inevitably forget details! Attention is like giving the translator a highlighting pen. Instead of memorizing everything at once, they look back and highlight the exact words they need for each word they write.

cards.title: Attention in Action
cards.body: When translating the long sentence 'The fluffy cat who slept on the rug all day finally woke up to eat,' an attention model doesn't try to cram all fifteen words into one tiny vector. Instead, when translating 'woke up', it dynamically focuses its attention back on 'cat' and 'slept'.

cards.title: Myth: Attention Slows Things Down
cards.body: Many people think attention makes translation slower because it does more work. Actually, by breaking the bottleneck, attention allows models to process words in parallel, making translation much faster and far more accurate for long documents!

cards.title: Bottleneck vs. Attention
cards.body: Compare how old systems and attention systems handle information flow.

left.text: Forces the entire sentence through one tiny, fixed-size vector. Info gets lost!

right.text: Creates a custom, dynamic context pathway for every word. No info is lost!

exercises.prompt: What is the main problem with squeezing an entire sentence into a single, fixed-size bottleneck vector?

options.text: It forgets the beginning of long sentences.
It makes the translation too fast.
It only works with numbers, not words.
It forces the model to use too many languages.

exercises.explain: A fixed-size bottleneck vector forces the model to compress the entire sentence into one small space, causing it to forget details in long sentences.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Attention models can translate long sentences much better than old bottleneck models.
exercises.explain: Attention allows the model to look back at any part of the input sentence, no matter how long it is, so it can translate very long sentences successfully.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how attention adapts to different parts of a sentence.
exercises.sentence: Instead of a fixed bottleneck, attention uses a/an [blank] context vector.
exercises.explain: Attention solves the bottleneck by creating a dynamic context vector that changes depending on which word is being translated.

exercises.prompt: Which of these systems is using attention to translate?

options.text: An AI that compresses a 50-word paragraph into a single 5-digit code before translating it.
An AI that looks back at the word 'apple' when translating the word 'pie' in a recipe.
An AI that translates word-for-word using a physical dictionary.

exercises.explain: The system that uses a dynamic context vector is using the attention mechanism to focus on specific words as needed.

exercises.prompt: How does the attention mechanism solve the information bottleneck problem in machine translation?

options.text: It creates a dynamic context vector for each word.
It makes the fixed-size bottleneck vector even smaller.
It forces the network to translate only short sentences.
It ignores the input sentence completely.

exercises.explain: Excellent job! By replacing the single, fixed bottleneck vector with dynamic context vectors, attention allows the network to handle sentences of any length. Next up: Attention Mechanics.
